features.summary: Apple reported Q2 FY2025 revenue of $95.4 billion, up 5% year-over-year, with diluted EPS of $1.65, up 8%. Services achieved an all-time record of $26.6 billion, growing 12% year-over-year. iPhone revenue was $46.8 billion (up 2%), Mac $7.9 billion (up 7%), iPad $6.4 billion (up 15%), and Wearables/Home/Accessories $7.5 billion (down 5%). The company announced a $500 billion U.S. investment plan over four years and returned $29 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.
features.management_outlook: Apple expects June quarter revenue to grow low to mid-single digits year-over-year, with gross margin between 45.5% and 46.5%. The company estimates $900 million in tariff-related costs for the June quarter, assuming current global tariff rates remain unchanged. Management noted that the majority of U.S.-bound iPhones will originate from India, with Vietnam as the origin for most other products sold in the U.S. Apple Intelligence features continue to roll out to additional languages and regions, though more personal Siri features are delayed as they work to meet quality standards.
features.risks: Tariff impacts represent the primary near-term risk, with $900 million estimated costs for June quarter and uncertainty beyond that period due to ongoing Section 232 investigations. Foreign exchange headwinds continue to pressure results by approximately 2.5 percentage points. Legal challenges including the Epic case injunction and ongoing DOJ antitrust cases pose potential risks to the Services business. Macroeconomic uncertainty and geopolitical tensions, particularly in China, create additional operational and demand risks.
features.key_drivers: Apple Intelligence adoption is driving stronger iPhone 16 performance in markets where it's available compared to regions without the feature. Services growth remains robust at 12% year-over-year with over 1 billion paid subscriptions and double-digit growth in paid accounts. Supply chain diversification continues with increased production in India and Vietnam reducing China dependency for U.S. sales. The installed base reached all-time highs across all product categories, providing a strong foundation for future Services growth and hardware upgrades.
